$comment: HIGH-RISK example — EU AI Act Annex III §5(b) credit scoring. Every field marked _REQUIRED_ must be accurate before visibility:public is accepted by the platform.
name: Credit Score Assistant
description: Assesses creditworthiness of individual loan applicants. Outputs a probability-of-default score between 0.0 and 1.0 with confidence interval.
intended_purpose: Credit risk assessment for personal loans up to EUR 50,000 granted to adults resident in EU-27. The agent supports, but does not replace, a qualified human underwriter.
known_limitations: Not validated for applicants resident outside EU-27
Requires at least 24 months of credit bureau history
Does not support mortgages, business loans, or revolving credit
Model accuracy degrades on incomes below EUR 1,200/month (underrepresented in training data)
Scores are probabilistic, not deterministic: a 0.80 score means 80% default probability under model assumptions, not a 'denied' decision
foreseeable_misuse: Using the score as a sole basis for loan rejection without human underwriter review (Art. 22 GDPR right to human review applies)
Applying to mortgages, credit lines, or any loan outside the EUR 50k personal loan scope
Chaining outputs into another automated system without preserving the confidence interval
eu_art4_literacy_notes: Loan officers using this agent must be trained that: (1) the output is a probability, not a decision; (2) confidence intervals matter — a 0.50 ± 0.30 score is NOT the same as a 0.50 ± 0.02 score; (3) the agent does not see protected attributes (age, sex, ethnicity), but some input features correlate with them — human review is required to spot proxy bias.
